parsed_review.summary: Claude Code Opus 4.8 max read-only review reported no P0, P1, or blocking P2 findings. It found the Matter trace slice correct, fail-closed, comprehensively tested, scope-controlled, and Hermes-evidence ready. P3 observations are forward-looking and non-blocking.

findings.title: requiredWhen metadata is declarative until later behavior slices
findings.disposition: Accepted as an explicit future-boundary note. S04 records requiredWhen metadata but does not claim required_when_enforced; enforcement belongs to later enum-policy, AI-output, finance/share/portal, or lifecycle slices when those executable triggers exist.

findings.title: Matter tenant authenticity depends on future data-access resolution
findings.disposition: Accepted as in-scope for this pure helper. S04 validates caller-provided Matter context consistency and cross-tenant mismatch. Authentic Matter-record lookup and tenant resolution are deferred to later data-access and Matter integration slices.

findings.title: Minor helper wording and redundant normalization are non-blocking
findings.disposition: No code change required for this subphase. Redundant validation plus normalization is retained as a defensive sibling pattern, and the thrown context error remains fail-closed.
